headline: Sungai Klang Rehabilitation RM4B Plan Shows Mixed Results After 5 Years
context: The River of Life project to rehabilitate Sungai Klang has spent RM4 billion since 2012 but achieved Class III water quality at only 42% of monitoring stations against a 70% target.

cards.big: The River of Life project promised to make Sungai Klang swimmable by 2020.
cards.sub: RM4B later, 58% of the river remains too polluted even for recreational contact.

cards.big: 78% of the RM4B was spent on beautification, not water treatment.
cards.sub: Landscaping and promenades are visible. Sewage interception — the core solution — received 22%.

cards.big: 1,200 sewage discharge points feed directly into Sungai Klang.
cards.sub: IWK has intercepted 340. The remaining 860 continue to discharge untreated effluent.

cards.big: Seoul's Cheonggyecheon restoration cost USD 900M and achieved Class II in 5 years.
cards.sub: Malaysia spent more in absolute terms on a longer river with worse results.

cards.big: Beautifying a polluted river is decorating the problem. The sewage is still there — it just has better lighting.
River rehabilitation works when the priority is water quality, not visual amenity. Sungai Klang needs sewage interception at scale, not more promenades.
